title: Spawn guard allows persistent team spawns (name + team_name); keeps denying one-shot Teammate copies
description: The spawn guard denies any Agent-tool call whose subagent_type names a rostered persistent Teammate, keying only on AGENT_TYPE_KEYS — it cannot distinguish a transient one-shot copy from a legitimate persistent-teammate spawn carrying name + team_name. Net effect observed live: the Team Lead spawning builder/packager into the team had to fall back to subagent_type general-purpose with the role inlined in the prompt, so rostered Teammates run WITHOUT their .claude/agents definitions. Fix in spawn-guard rules/logic: when the spawn payload carries both name and team_name (a persistent team spawn), allow it even for a rostered type; continue denying bare one-shot spawns of rostered types with the existing redirect reason. Update the deny reason to name the allowed mechanism (spawn with name + team_name into the team).
acceptance_criteria: A spawn payload with subagent_type = rostered Teammate AND both name and team_name passes the guard.
A spawn payload with subagent_type = rostered Teammate and no team_name (or no name) is denied with a redirect reason that names the persistent-spawn mechanism.
Non-rostered types pass unchanged; fail-safe exit 0 on malformed config/payload preserved.
Rules stay data-only (vocabulary in spawn-guard-rules.cjs), decision logic in spawn-guard.cjs.
Verified with payload simulations covering allow/deny/fail-safe; ships as .cjs in template + dogfood mirror; passes code-standards and package-qa gates.
Released to npm in the next version (0.5.2).
root_cause: spawn-guard keyed only on AGENT_TYPE_KEYS, so it could not distinguish a persistent team spawn (name + team_name) from a transient one-shot copy of a rostered Teammate.
fix: Guard allows a rostered type when the payload carries both non-empty name and team_name; one-shots and partial payloads stay denied with a reason naming the mechanism. Commit e5bcf81 after code-standards and package-qa PASS.
